channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Adelaide man to face court over alleged fuel drive-offs using trucks
item.description: A man has been charged for allegedly using trucks in serial petrol drive-offs north of Adelaide, with police estimating the cost to be more than $8,000.

media:group.media:description: A man allegedly used trucks to steal fuel in Adelaide's north over a period of seven months.

item.title: 'Those guys are gonna disappear': ICAC reveals 'Pink Ladies' texts
item.description: Messages obtained by the NSW corruption watchdog have revealed discussions by a group of long-time Sydney local government officials about plans to secure senior positions for themselves and friends at City of Parramatta Council.
item.dc:creator: Nabil Al-Nashar

media:group.media:description: Angela Jones-Blayney told the corruption watchdog she was a long-time member of the “Pink Ladies”, a group who worked together at various Sydney councils.

item.title: AFP defends public arrest of Ben Roberts-Smith
item.description: The Australian Federal Police has detailed intelligence surrounding Ben Roberts-Smith's arrest, pushing back against criticisms of its public nature.
item.dc:creator: Tom Lowrey

media:group.media:description: AFP Commissioner Krissy Barrett detailed Ben Roberts-Smith's arrest following questions about its public nature.

item.title: People trapped in new housing estate traffic bottlenecks welcome funding
item.description: Motorists in Melbourne's outer north welcome funds for "lifeline" outer suburbs road, but say more needs to be done.
item.dc:creator: Margaret Paul

media:group.media:description: Mustafa Aljburi says driving is a daily headache for local motorists.

item.title: Ex-police officer admits sharing bed but denies rape, court hears
item.description: A former South Australian police officer says he got into bed with a woman while she was sleeping and caressed her thigh soon after she rejected his advances, but denies raping her, a court has heard.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Carbone

media:group.media:description: The former police officer is facing trial in South Australia's District Court.

item.title: Young police officer's death raises questions about mental health support
item.description: An inquest into the death of a NSW Police recruit who was less than three months into the job hears differing opinions about whether it was related to his work.
item.dc:creator: Catherine James

media:group.media:description: An inquest into the self-inflicted death of a new police recruit has explored mental health support in the force.

item.title: Live: Saints' tough loss made worse as midfielder injures Achilles
item.description: Hawthorn consolidates its top four spot with a big win over St Kilda, as the Saints lose Sam Flanders to a potentially serious Achilles injury. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Andrew McGarry

media:group.media:description: Sam Flanders' season looks over after the St Kilda midfielder suffered a suspected Achilles injury against Hawthorn at Docklands.

item.title: Australian to join 'scary as hell' mission to rescue Laos villagers from cave
item.description: A diver describes the "emotional moment" when he found a group of men alive after they were trapped for more than a week in a Laos cave, while a South Australian renowned for his ability to navigate tight spaces joins the rescue mission.
item.dc:creator: Tim Swanston and Josh Brine

media:group.media:description: Mikko Paasi climbing backwards to locate the group in the Laos cave.

item.title: Live: Blues storm to historic Women's Origin clean sweep
item.description: The Women's State of Origin series wraps up on the Gold Coast, with NSW completing a clean sweep at a rain-soaked Robina Stadium on the Gold Coast. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Jayme Fressard had a try taken off her in the first half, but got over legitimately in the second.

item.title: NRL backs referee's decision to send off Ponga in Origin I
item.description: The NRL supports Ashley Klein's controversial decision to send off Kalyn Ponga, saying the State of Origin referee got the call right.

media:group.media:description: Ashley Klein (centre) sent Kalyn Ponga (left) from the field in the second half of Origin I.

item.title: 'Lost opportunity' to help air force serviceman before he died, inquest finds
item.description: A coroner has found that the death of an RAAF serviceman could have been prevented, including when he left a military health centre during a mental health crisis. Warning: This story contains discussion of suicide and mental health issues.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: An inquest into the death of RAAF aircraftman Gary Sargeant found his drowning was preventable.

item.title: Work about to begin on $3.6b Olympic Stadium as protesters vow to stay put
item.description: The Queensland government wants site work to begin on Brisbane's $3.6 billion Olympic stadium on Monday as residents plan a court challenge and Indigenous protesters refuse to move.
item.dc:creator: Annie Guest

media:group.media:description: An artist's impression of the proposed Olympic stadium at Brisbane's Victoria Park.

item.title: Darwin mum's killer ordered to spend indefinite period in custody
item.description: Sheena Fairfield was stabbed to death outside her family home more than three years ago. Her killer will now spend 20 years in supervised custody.
item.dc:creator: Olivana Lathouris

media:group.media:description: Joerg Kloepper will spend an indefinite period in a mental health facility over the killing of Darwin mum Sheena Fairfield.

item.title: 'Destroys souls': Coercive control legislation tabled in ACT but laws still years away
item.description: The government's bill does not detail specific coercive control behaviours which it says is deliberate.
item.dc:creator: Tahlia Roy and Georgia Roberts

media:group.media:description: Canberra Liberals spokesperson for women Leanne Castley has called for coercive control laws for years.

item.title: Victoria's political donations cap to double to $10,000 under proposed changes
item.description: The cap on political donations in Victoria will be at least doubled and public funding for political parties increased under government proposals to rewrite the regime struck out by the High Court.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Eddie

media:group.media:description: Labor is seeking to lift the cap on donations to $10,000 as it attempts to remake Victoria's electoral laws.

item.title: ABC appoints Reuters exec Simon Robinson as news director
item.description: Senior Reuters executive Simon Robinson takes over from former director Justin Stevens who announced his resignation yesterday after four years in the job.

media:group.media:description: Simon Robinson will be the ABC news director from September.

item.title: Greens push for minister's resignation after legal fee revelation
item.description: Madeleine Ogilvie is being accused of deliberately misleading parliament, after she revealed her involvement in a Supreme Court matter, months after saying she was not connected to any legal action.
item.dc:creator: Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: Madeleine Ogilvie is being put under pressure to resign, amid accusations she misled parliament over taxpayer-funded legal action.

item.title: Nationals pull election stunt as tax debate heats up
item.description: This week a few people got a grilling at Senate estimates, the tax debate heated up and whispers of a new centre-right party fluttered through the press gallery.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: Nationals Leader Matt Canavan today walked out the front of Parliament House with a bold declaration: an election should be called.

item.title: Outrage after women on history walk harassed by boys on e-bikes
item.description: Nine women say they were encircled, yelled at and filmed by a group of teenage boys on e-bikes while on a women's history tour of Wollongong.
item.dc:creator: Penny Burfitt

media:group.media:description: A group of teenagers on e-bikes allegedly interrupted and harassed a group of women on a historical walking tour.

item.title: Alleged murderer's bail in limbo as Tiwi Islands town erupts in riots
item.description: Lawyers prepare legal action against a Tiwi Islands town manager who blocked an alleged murderer from being granted bail to the islands, where his potential return has already provoked rioting.
item.dc:creator: Roxanne Fitzgerald

media:group.media:description: Justin Bukulatjpi was granted bail to live with family on the Tiwi Islands in May.

item.title: Young finds method in the madness for long-awaited Origin arrival
item.description: It took three years and four series, but when Hudson Young was given the chance to impose his unorthodox abilities on State of Origin football, he didn't hesitate to harness the chaos for New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: Young had his best game for New South Wales in the Blues' Game I victory.

item.title: Supercars ref sorry for calling driver 'a grub' over controversial crash
item.description: The Supercars referee offers an apology to a driver after describing him as "a bit of a grub" because of his actions at the conclusion of last year's championship.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Ryan Wood's crash with Broc Feeney in the Supercars final last year helped his teammate win the championship.

item.title: Coroner finds smuggled drugs cause of inmate's death
item.description: A 35-year-old inmate at Victoria's Fulham Correctional Centre died of a drug overdose after consuming drugs smuggled into the jail, a coronial investigation has found.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Stuchbery

media:group.media:description: The Fulham Correctional Centre is located near Sale in Victoria's east.

item.title: Lawyers expect will disputes to escalate in coming decades
item.description: A significant proportion of us believe that an inheritance will be the key to our future financial security, but most families have not even discussed what is in the will, and 40 per cent do not have one at all, a recent survey shows.
item.dc:creator: Kate Leaver and Nadia Mitsopoulos

media:group.media:description: 90 per cent of baby boomers and 95 per cent of those aged over 80 have made a will.

item.title: Victim of suspicious unit fire in Canberra identified
item.description: ACT Policing is treating the death of Kylee McHugh, 46, as a homicide and calling for people to come forward if they had recently seen her.

media:group.media:description: Forty-six-year-old Kylee McHugh died in a blaze in her ground-floor unit.
